9. Sookshma Lagna and BT rectification
The concept here is that "/fwe multiply the lagna longitude
at BTwith 972, the resultant longitude should be the longitude
of Moon at BT or its trines. The formula is- Lagna longitude
x 972 = Moon's longitude or its trines. Saravali and Brihat
Jataka supports this method".

78 __________________- =~S~e~c~~e~~~o~(~B~ir~th~n~·m~e~R~e~ct~ifi~zc~a~tio~n
This is a method interpreted based on the sloka
'i!i#f4tlli:J:ifiJ<tk'Q#;' (Bora -lti-Ahotatra-Vikalpam-Eke) present
in Brihat Jataka. If we add the digits indicated by the word
'Ahoratra' as per KTPY notation, we get, 8+2+2 = 12. As per
KTPY notation 'lti' means 60. Thus it means that 'Ahoratra'
is 60 Khati. If we divide this 60 with 12, we get 5 Khati,
which is the average span of a sign. On dividing this into 4
we get 125 Khati. Divide this by 9, and we get Sookshma
